reviews.review_msg: Dr.Pio is by far one of the most unbearable teachers I've ever had. His accent makes it far too difficult to understand during lecture and his monotone voice doesn't help that cause. Tests are solely based off lecture content so there's no point in showing up. Just study his slides and you'll do fine. Definitely prepare to teach yourself

yes he has a heavy French accent but his tests are identical to what you learn in the lecture slides/practice tests/tutorial worksheets. We did have to purchase a textbook but I did not read it and got close to 100% on all my tests just by studying the slides. 3 quizzes a week online, open book, like 3-7 questions. 
Tests are all non cumulative :D

Professor Pio was a nice guy, but clearly not interested in teaching. He seemed bored by the material, read in a monotone voice and basically read straight from the slides. The best way I can describe his extremely heavy accent is that it felt like I had the subtitles in front of me and yet still couldn't decipher what he was trying to say.

Exams were very fair. Final exam was cumulative but manageable as he gave many hints during review sessions. He was very accessible for extra help. He will explain concepts over and over again until he is convinced you actually understand.

Virtually impossible to understand and not at all any more helpful during his office hours. Exams are reasonable other than the 2 questions he'll teach incorrectly or take from lectures that aren't supposed to be included. One of the worst professors I've ever had.

Good luck trying to understand him. Attend lectures for the iclickers and self-teach the rest. Follow the SLOs on the lecture slides and look at diagrams in the textbook to do well on the exams. His practice exams are very similar to the midterms and finals. Overall not a very tough prof if you can learn the material on your own.
The midterm & final are from the notes Very fair. Very knowledgeable & the accent youll get used to I. A few days

Don't know why everyone hates on him so much, he isn't the most interesting prof and he has an accent but he doesn't deserve all the flack that he gets. He's a nice guy and exams are relatively easy, plus they're marked fair. He tries many times to be engaging, it's just that most people in this course(myself included) don't care much about MBB..
He is the worst port I have ever seen.

He has a thick French accent but you eventually get used to it. Not as bad as people say, though not great either. Lectures are boring but the material he covers in class is usually the stuff that will show up on quizzes or midterms.

He's not THAT bad of a professor as people say. The accent - you'll get used to after a week. His lectures are boring but the midterm and final were straight from his notes. His quizzes are not hard either, you just have to read the textbook and teach yourself what material is relevant from it by looking at his learning outcomes.

exams and quizzes ridiculously hard. he does not test main concepts. make sure you know random, small details because that is what is going to be on the exams. you do not need to show up to lectures, you will not be able to understand him. read the textbook. only way you will learn
Terrible prof, can't understand him, only reason to go to lecture is for participation mark. He has a thick french accent, only way to learn is from the textbook. Lecture slides are terrible
Mbb 201? More like French 201. Lectures only mandatory due to iclickers and quizzes.

A researcher maybe, an instructor? No. Was not able to convey ideas clearly and focused too heavily on memorization especially considering the course was supposed to be concept based. I ended up getting an A- without ever taking a single note in his class, relying purely on self-study sessions.

Prof is really passionate about his field, and I believe works hard to ensure the success of his students. He tries to make this class seem interesting, as material is quite dry. Notes are detailed but not overly so. That being said, it's unfortunate that his thick french accent makes it very hard to understand him. Avoid if you can't self study.

If you have trouble paying attention in class or making your own notes I advise to stay away from this prof. The TA in this class is what saved my grade and he most likely wrote the exams which were ok. I am not sure if this prof will teach again as there were other profs in his lectures marking what he was doing which is never good. Thick accent!
Decent prof. Hard to understand but it doesnt matter just study on your own and youll be fine. exams are straight from lecture notes, problem sets and tutorial questions.

Avoid this prof if you can. Lectures were extremely boring due to his monotone voice and French accent. Lecture slides get posted before lecture and are complete, but they are basically copy and pasted from the textbook and lengthy.. definitely not 'notes'. Seems like he's passionate about the material, but not interested in teaching.

Dr. Pio is a decent professor, he knows his stuff, and he's very intellectual with his teaching. He's not the one who makes up the exams, it's his TA's. With that said, the course material is very dense and dry, and you need to study in order to understand. The TA's do a good job of conferring exam-related info. Other than that, course is okay.

AT ALL COSTS, DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HIM. He may be the worst prof I have ever encountered, in terms of teaching ability and understanding. His french accent is such a determent, which can be incredibly hard for many people to understand.  His ability to imply the concept of each lecture is terribly weak.

Save yourself the time and DO NOT TAKE THE CLASS WITH HIM! Terrible prof, cannot understand him at all in class and makes material very confusing. His notes are impossible to understand which makes it even worse... The guy seems like he just wants to do research and doesn't care about teaching

A lot of students judge Dr. Pio very unfairly. If you take the time to sit at the front of the class and talk to him outside of lectures you will see that he is a very knowledgable and helpful teacher. I had no problem understanding his French accent (I'm not French).

He might not be a very good prof,but this course can still earn you very good grades,he's really nice and tries to help as much as he is capable of,the contents are actually quite helpful for research careers
a very nice prof, willing to help. But his lectures were extremely boring and dry. It was not easy to understand him due to his heavy accent.

Extremely thick French accent --- takes a while to get used to but you understand it over time. Clearly knows the material well but not so good at communicating it. Lectures and midterms were textbook-based. Easy presentation marker.
If you are fluent in French, then he is the prof for you ; otherwise ... hire a translator!
